title: hibbertia cistiflora subsp. cistiflora
intro: Hibbertia cistiflora subsp. cistiflora, also known as the 'Native Guelder Rose', is a flowering plant species native to Australia. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful for treating skin issues and wounds. The health benefits of Hibbertia cistiflora subsp. cistiflora include its potential to soothe and calm the skin, as well as its ability to support the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant is often made into a tincture, tea, or salve to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health issues.
